Tests uploaded by Trevis Autotest2 from trevis-35vm9 for review-ldiskfs received by maloo, run on CentOS 7.0/x86_64: (https://testing.whamcloud.com/test_sessions/430eb63f-9379-425c-9d91-aca738985564). Ran 7 tests.  1 tests failed: sanity.

Patch Set 7:

Shilong,
for future reference I would recommend to NOT retrigger the testing on this patch just for testing purposes, especially if this patch is fixing an important bug, since that test failed for some other reason (unrelated, LU-11183), and then we couldn't land it until the testing has passed.  Luckily, Oleg landed the patch before the test had failed. 

It would be better to checkout an exact copy of this patch using the "Download->Checkout" menu item in the top right corner of the patch window (do *not* use "Cherry Pick" as that would cause this patch to be rebased and lose all test results), then create a new trivial patch on top of this one (e.g. do space->tab conversion in a file), and add:

  Test-Parameters: trivial testlist=sanity
  Test-Parameters: trivial testlist=sanity
  Test-Parameters: trivial testlist=sanity
  Test-Parameters: trivial testlist=sanity

to the commit message (each line will run in a separate session).  This adds better testing, and also avoids changing the original patch, so it can be landed without problems.

Patch Set 6:

Tests uploaded by Trevis Autotest2 from trevis-33vm1 for custom received by maloo, run on CentOS 7.0/x86_64: (https://testing.whamcloud.com/test_sessions/aae9e680-e6c3-4c8d-9660-941f40da2eff). Ran 11 tests. No failures.

Patch Set 7:

Andreas, thanks for pointing it out, got it.

Patch Set 7:

Note that a patch like this should NOT be using the "trivial" keyword. That reduces testing coverage, and code changes can have unintended consequences in other parts of the code.

Patch Set 5:

To follow up on this, I now see that the "trivial" keyword was only added in v6 of the patch for extra testing, after v5 had already passed all of the testing.  The mechanism of requesting the additional testing was incorrect, as previously mentioned, but there was no gap in test coverage due to this.

parents.subject: LU-11006 lnet: fix show peer yaml tree with no peer

commit.subject: LU-11279 lod: reset ostlist properly in lod_get_default_lov_striping
commit.message:
LU-11279 lod: reset ostlist properly in lod_get_default_lov_striping

Ostlist might be allocated previously, and we should
reset them properly, otherwise, it will pollute new
default setting and cause unexpected behavior.

Test-Parameters: trivial testlist=sanity,sanity,sanity,sanity,sanity,sanity,sanity,sanity,sanity
Change-Id: I9b7acb5f05ec4b371da99f68b9647f0b75cd7021
Signed-off-by: Wang Shilong <wshilong@ddn.com>
Reviewed-on: https://review.whamcloud.com/33069
Reviewed-by: Andreas Dilger <adilger@whamcloud.com>
Reviewed-by: Bobi Jam <bobijam@hotmail.com>
Tested-by: Jenkins
Tested-by: Maloo <hpdd-maloo@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: Oleg Drokin <green@whamcloud.com>
